channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Doctors warn against using 'parasite cleanses' made popular by wellness influencers
item.description: Some wellness influencers think many people have parasites and should use special supplements to flush them out. Is there science behind the claims?
item.content:encoded: Some wellness influencers think many people have parasites and should use special supplements to flush them out. Is there science behind the claims?
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden

item.title: 'Participatory defense' helps people take a direct role in their own criminal defense
item.description: Groups that support people facing criminal charges aren't new. But these participatory defense hubs, as they're called, have grown significantly in the past decade.
item.content:encoded: Groups that support people facing criminal charges aren't new. But these participatory defense hubs, as they're called, have grown significantly in the past decade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Lehr

item.title: Brooklyn baker Tanya Bush on her new cookbook, 'Will This Make You Happy'
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Tanya Bush about her new cookbook. She writes about a tumultuous year in her life filled with challenges and self discovery -- through baking.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Tanya Bush about her new cookbook. She writes about a tumultuous year in her life filled with challenges and self discovery -- through baking.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: What are Trump's options in Iran as oil stops flowing and allies resist joining war?
item.description: Iran has effectively shut down the flow of oil from the Gulf region. President Trump hasn't offered a clear solution. Trump is calling for allies to assist the U.S. military in removing the Iranian threat, and many of the allies are resisting.
item.content:encoded: Iran has effectively shut down the flow of oil from the Gulf region. President Trump hasn't offered a clear solution. Trump is calling for allies to assist the U.S. military in removing the Iranian threat, and many of the allies are resisting.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: Here are the names of the 6 Air Force airmen who died when their refueling plane crashed
item.description: On Saturday, the U.S. Defense Department released the names of six service members who died when their military refueling aircraft crashed.
item.content:encoded: On Saturday, the U.S. Defense Department released the names of six service members who died when their military refueling aircraft crashed.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: How a gift of $50 was life-changing for this daughter of a Miami taxi driver
item.description: A daughter tells a story about her father, a taxi driver in Miami, who talked up her ambition to a repeat customer and got $50 specifically for her -- just enough for a life-changing opportunity.
item.content:encoded: A daughter tells a story about her father, a taxi driver in Miami, who talked up her ambition to a repeat customer and got $50 specifically for her -- just enough for a life-changing opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: A new tool is helping to annihilate dangerous PFAS in firefighting foam
item.description: For decades, firefighters used a foam that contained PFAS, or forever chemicals, that can cause cancer and other illnesses. Now a "PFAS Annihilator" can destroy the toxic chemical in the foam.
item.content:encoded: For decades, firefighters used a foam that contained PFAS, or forever chemicals, that can cause cancer and other illnesses. Now a "PFAS Annihilator" can destroy the toxic chemical in the foam.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Martinez-Smiley

item.title: New data-sharing rules for Medicaid cause fears among patients who are immigrants
item.description: When Medicaid began sharing personal data with federal immigration authorities last year, it upended decades of explicit promises to patients. Now, even eligible immigrants fear enrolling.
item.content:encoded: When Medicaid began sharing personal data with federal immigration authorities last year, it upended decades of explicit promises to patients. Now, even eligible immigrants fear enrolling.
item.dc:creator: Alex Olgin

item.title: New guidelines for managing cholesterol recommend an additional blood test
item.description: New guidelines for managing cholesterol call for a more aggressive preventive approach, which could translate into more Americans being treated with medicines and a stronger focus on lifestyle.
item.content:encoded: New guidelines for managing cholesterol call for a more aggressive preventive approach, which could translate into more Americans being treated with medicines and a stronger focus on lifestyle.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: HUNTR/X went from a complete unknown to winning a Grammy, and now, an Oscar
item.description: The band Huntr/x from the hit Netflix film "KPop Demon Hunters" went from a group no one had ever heard of to one of the biggest pop acts of 2025. Now their hit 'Golden' has won a Grammy and an Oscar.
item.content:encoded: The band Huntr/x from the hit Netflix film "KPop Demon Hunters" went from a group no one had ever heard of to one of the biggest pop acts of 2025. Now their hit 'Golden' has won a Grammy and an Oscar.
item.dc:creator: Ailsa Chang
